title: OpenAI Releases GPT-3
summary: OpenAI released GPT-3, a 175-billion parameter language model that demonstrated remarkable few-shot and zero-shot capabilities. Without any fine-tuning, GPT-3 could perform translation, question answering, code generation, and creative writing simply by being given a few examples in the prompt. The paper 'Language Models are Few-Shot Learners' showed that scaling model size produced emergent capabilities that smaller models lacked. GPT-3 was the first model to make the general public — and the tech industry — take notice that language models might be approaching general-purpose intelligence.
impact_description: Proved that scaling alone produces qualitatively new capabilities. Established in-context learning as a paradigm. Triggered the scaling race among AI labs and convinced the industry that large language models were the path to general AI capabilities.
counterfactual: Without GPT-3, the scaling hypothesis might not have been validated so dramatically. The AI industry's multi-billion dollar bet on large language models might have been delayed. ChatGPT would not have existed in its form, and the generative AI explosion of 2022-2024 might have been significantly different.
query_hooks: When did large language models become impressive?
Why did AI labs start building bigger models?
